howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.subtitle: 4 simple steps to your new employee or job
howItWorks.step1Desc: Company or applicant: registration is free and only takes a few minutes.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Companies post job offers, applicants create their profile with qualifications.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Automatic matching connects the right profiles — applications handled directly in the portal.
howItWorks.step4Desc: Need more support? IJP helps with candidate selection, visa processes and onboarding.

whyJobon.title: Why JobOn?
whyJobon.subtitle: Your benefits at a glance
whyJobon.freeDesc: Companies post for free, applicants register and apply for free.
whyJobon.personalDesc: Candidates from all over the world — including international professionals already living in Germany.
whyJobon.visaDesc: Optional: IJP supports with visa processes, recognition and administrative tasks.
whyJobon.partnersDesc: No middlemen. Companies and applicants communicate directly through the portal.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Answers to the most important questions
faq.a1: Yes, posting job offers and receiving applications is free for companies. Only the optional IJP full service is paid.
faq.q2: What is the difference to traditional recruitment?
faq.a2: JobOn is a job board: companies and applicants find each other directly. Optionally, companies or applicants can hire IJP as a personal recruiter — that is an additional service.
faq.q3: Do I need German skills as an applicant?
faq.a3: It depends on the position. For many positions in kitchen, housekeeping or logistics, little or no German is required.
faq.q4: Do you help with finding accommodation?
faq.a4: Many partner companies offer housing. With the optional IJP service we also actively support with finding accommodation.
faq.q5: Which industries are represented on JobOn?
faq.a5: Gastronomy, hospitality, logistics, cleaning and more. Other industries can also post positions.

finalCta.title: Get started — for free
finalCta.description: Register as a company or applicant and connect directly. Optional: hire IJP as a recruiter.

positionTypeDescriptions.studentenferienjob: For students from abroad who want to work in Germany during semester breaks. Maximum 90 days (3 months) per year. Requirement: Active enrollment at a foreign university.
positionTypeDescriptions.saisonjob: Short-term contingent employment for seasonal work in agriculture, hospitality or tourism. Maximum 90 days per year. Applies to persons from third countries with work permit.
positionTypeDescriptions.workandholiday: Working Holiday Visa for young people (18-30 years) from partner countries like Argentina, Chile, Australia, etc. Allows working and traveling in Germany for up to 12 months.
positionTypeDescriptions.fachkraft: For qualified workers with recognized vocational training or university degree. Long-term employment with skilled worker visa possible.
positionTypeDescriptions.ausbildung: For people who want to complete vocational training (apprenticeship) in Germany. Dual training with vocational school and practical work in the company. Duration: 2-3.5 years.

stellenarten.title: Job Types Overview
stellenarten.subtitle: Discover the different ways to work in Germany. We explain the differences and help you make the right choice.
stellenarten.notSure: Not sure which job type fits?
stellenarten.notSureDesc: Our team is happy to advise you and help you make the right decision. Contact us for personal consultation.

studentenferienjob.title: Student Holiday Job
studentenferienjob.subtitle: For students during semester breaks
studentenferienjob.shortDesc: Earn money during your semester break and gain valuable work experience in Germany.
studentenferienjob.description: Student holiday jobs are ideal for students from abroad who want to work in Germany during their semester breaks. You can work up to 90 days or 180 half days per year and gain valuable experience.
forWhom: Good German skills are an advantage
docs: Work permit (depending on country of origin)

saisonjob.title: Seasonal Job
saisonjob.subtitle: Seasonal employment
saisonjob.shortDesc: Work in agriculture, tourism, or other seasonal industries.
saisonjob.description: Seasonal jobs offer flexible employment opportunities in industries with seasonal labor demand. Typical sectors include agriculture, viticulture, tourism, and hospitality.
forWhom: Job seekers from EU and third countries

workandholiday.title: Work & Holiday
workandholiday.subtitle: Combine work and travel
workandholiday.shortDesc: Combine work and vacation in Germany with the Work & Holiday visa.
workandholiday.description: The Work & Holiday program allows young people to travel Germany and work at the same time. Ideal for those who want to combine culture and work experience.

fachkraft.title: Skilled Worker
fachkraft.subtitle: Qualified employment
fachkraft.shortDesc: Start your career as a qualified skilled worker in Germany.
fachkraft.description: As a skilled worker, you have the opportunity to take up a permanent position in Germany. We place qualified specialists in various industries with the prospect of long-term employment.
forWhom: Completed vocational training or university degree

ausbildung.title: Vocational Training
ausbildung.subtitle: Dual vocational training
ausbildung.shortDesc: Start a dual vocational training in Germany and earn a recognized professional qualification.
ausbildung.description: The dual vocational training in Germany combines practical work in a company with theoretical instruction at a vocational school. You receive a salary and an internationally recognized degree.
forWhom: School leaving certificate (at least lower secondary)

about.title: About JobOn
about.subtitle: We connect international talent with German companies - for successful collaboration across borders.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.text1: JobOn was developed by IJP - International Job Placement to pave the way for international professionals and students to Germany while providing German companies access to motivated and qualified workers.
mission.text2: We understand the challenges on both sides and offer a platform that makes the entire placement process transparent, efficient and personal.

services.seasonalDesc: Seasonal employment in agriculture, hospitality and other industries.
services.skilledDesc: Placement of qualified professionals for permanent employment in Germany.
services.trainingDesc: Training positions for young talents who want to start their career in Germany.

overview.title: Privacy at a Glance
overview.subtitle: General Information
overview.text: The following information provides a simple overview of what happens to your personal data when you visit this website.

responsible.title: Responsible Party
responsible.text: The responsible party is the natural or legal person who alone or jointly with others determines the purposes and means of processing personal data.

collection.title: Data Collection on this Website
collection.who: Who is responsible for data collection?
collection.whoText: Data processing on this website is carried out by the website operator.
collection.how: How do we collect your data?
collection.howText1: Your data is collected when you provide it to us.
collection.howText2: Other data is automatically collected by our IT systems when you visit the website.
collection.why: What do we use your data for?
collection.whyText: Part of the data is collected to ensure error-free provision of the website.

rights.title: Your Rights
rights.text: You have the right at any time to:
rights.access: Obtain information about your stored data

registration.title: Registration and User Account
registration.text: You can register on our website as an applicant or company.

sharing.title: Data Sharing
sharing.text: As a placement platform, we share your applicant data with potential employers upon your express consent.

storage.title: Storage Duration
storage.text: We store your data as long as your user account is active.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.text: Our website uses technically necessary cookies.

ssl.title: SSL Encryption
ssl.text: This site uses SSL encryption for security reasons.

changes.title: Changes to this Privacy Policy
changes.text: We reserve the right to adapt this privacy policy.

jobCategories.title: Job Types Overview
jobCategories.subtitle: Germany offers various opportunities for international workers. Find out which option suits you best.
jobCategories.unsureDesc: Contact us and we'll help you find the right job type for your situation.

studentenferienjob.title: Student Summer Job
studentenferienjob.subtitle: Max. 90 days during semester break
studentenferienjob.shortDesc: Ideal for students who want to work in Germany during semester breaks.
studentenferienjob.description: The Student Summer Job allows students from non-EU countries to work in Germany for up to 90 days during semester breaks. A simplified work permit is issued through the Federal Employment Agency.

saisonjob.title: Seasonal Job
saisonjob.subtitle: Up to 8 months seasonal work
saisonjob.shortDesc: For those who want to work seasonally in Germany long-term.
saisonjob.description: Seasonal work allows employment for up to 8 months in certain industries. Ideal for agriculture, tourism, and gastronomy in peak and off-peak seasons.

workandholiday.title: Work & Holiday
workandholiday.subtitle: Combine work and travel
workandholiday.shortDesc: For young people who want to explore Germany while working.
workandholiday.description: The Work & Holiday program is aimed at young people from countries with bilateral agreements with Germany. It allows you to work in Germany for up to a year while traveling the country.
workandholiday.forWhom2: From partner countries (e.g., Australia, New Zealand)

fachkraft.title: Skilled Worker
fachkraft.subtitle: Qualified employment
fachkraft.shortDesc: For trained professionals with recognized qualifications.
fachkraft.description: As a skilled worker, you can work long-term in Germany in your trained profession. The prerequisite is the recognition of your foreign professional qualification in Germany.

ausbildung.title: Ausbildung
ausbildung.subtitle: Vocational training in Germany
ausbildung.shortDesc: Learn a profession with the German dual education system.
ausbildung.description: The dual training combines practical work in a company with theoretical lessons at vocational school. After 2-3.5 years, you receive a recognized professional qualification.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find answers to the most important questions about IJP and our placement services.
faq.moreQuestionsDesc: Our team is happy to answer all your questions personally.

employer.q1: How do I register my company?
employer.a1:
Registration is simple and free:

1. Click on "Register" in the navigation
2. Select "Register as a company"
3. Enter your email address and a secure password
4. Complete your company profile with contact details and description
5. You can create job listings immediately
employer.q2: How do I create a job listing?
employer.a2:
After registering and logging in:

1. Go to "My Job Listings" in the dashboard
2. Click on "Create New Job"
3. Fill in all relevant fields:
   - Job title and description
   - Requirements (language skills, qualifications)
   - Location and salary
   - Job type (Skilled worker, Ausbildung, Seasonal job, etc.)
4. Publish the job

Tip: The more detailed your information, the more suitable applicants you'll receive.
employer.q3: What costs are there for employers?
employer.a3:
Our pricing model:

• Posting job listings: Free
• Viewing applicant profiles: Free
• Successful placement: Commission only upon successful hiring

We're happy to discuss the exact terms individually with you. Contact us for a non-binding offer.
employer.q4: How does the application process work?
employer.a4:
The process is transparent and efficient:

1. Application received: Applicant applies for your position
2. IJP review: We check the documents for completeness
3. Forwarding: Suitable candidates are forwarded to you
4. Your decision: You review the applications and invite for interviews
5. Hiring: If interested, you hire the candidate

You have access to the status of all applications in your dashboard at any time.
employer.q5: What documents do I receive from the applicant?
employer.a5:
Depending on the job type, you receive:

For all applicants:
• Resume (CV)
• Passport photo
• Passport/ID copy
• Language certificates (if available)

For skilled workers additionally:
• Professional degree/diploma
• Employment references
• Qualification certificates

For students/seasonal workers:
• Enrollment certificate
• University certificate

All documents are checked by us for authenticity and completeness.
employer.q6: Which countries do the applicants come from?
employer.a6:
Our applicants mainly come from:

• Eastern Europe: Ukraine, Moldova, Georgia, Armenia
• Central Asia: Uzbekistan, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan
• Other countries: On request

All applicants have the necessary requirements for employment in Germany or we support in obtaining the necessary permits.

applicant.a1:
It's that easy:

1. Register for free as an applicant
2. Complete your profile with all relevant data
3. Upload your documents (resume, certificates, etc.)
4. Browse available job listings
5. Apply with one click

Alternatively, you can "Request IJP" - then we actively search for suitable positions for you.
applicant.a2:
The required documents depend on the job type:

Student Summer Job:
• Passport
• Enrollment certificate (with translation)
• Passport photo
• BA declaration

Skilled Worker:
• Passport
• Resume
• Professional degree/diploma
• Employment references
• Language certificate (recommended)

Ausbildung:
• Passport
• School certificate
• Resume
• Language certificate (min. B1)

All documents should be uploaded as PDF.
applicant.a3:
Yes, our service is completely free for applicants!

We finance ourselves through placement commissions paid by employers. There are no costs for you as an applicant - neither for registration, nor for application or placement.
applicant.q4: What language skills do I need?
applicant.a4:
The requirements vary depending on the position:

Student Summer Job/Seasonal Job:
• German: A2-B1 (basic knowledge sufficient)
• English: Helpful, but not mandatory

Skilled Worker:
• German: B1-B2 (depending on profession)
• Profession-specific certificates may be required

Ausbildung:
• German: At least B1
• B2 is recommended for vocational school

Tip: The better your German skills, the more job opportunities are available to you.
applicant.a5:
With this function, you commission us to actively search for suitable positions for you:

Advantages:
• We search our network for suitable positions
• You are informed about new opportunities
• Personal support from our team
• Access to non-publicly advertised positions

How it works:
1. Complete your profile
2. Select your desired job type(s)
3. Click on "Request IJP"
4. We actively search and contact you when we find suitable offers

legal.q1: Do I need a visa for Germany?
legal.a1:
This depends on your nationality:

EU citizens: No visa required, free labor market access

Third-country nationals:
• For short-term employment (max. 90 days): Often work permit through Federal Employment Agency
• For longer-term employment: Work visa required

We support you in applying for the necessary permits and advise on the requirements.
legal.a2:
Short-term employment is a special regulation:

• Duration: Maximum 90 days or 3 months per calendar year
• For: Students from certain countries (e.g., Ukraine, Moldova)
• Advantage: Simplified procedure without residence permit

Requirements:
• Valid enrollment certificate
• Placement through licensed agency (like IJP)
• Approval from Federal Employment Agency

We take care of all formalities!
legal.q3: What working hours apply in Germany?
legal.a3:
The following regulations apply in Germany:

• Maximum working time: 8 hours/day, max. 10 hours with compensation
• Breaks: After 6 hours min. 30 min., after 9 hours min. 45 min.
• Rest period: Min. 11 hours between working days
• Weekend: Generally work-free (exceptions in certain industries)

Minimum wage (2025): €13.90/hour
legal.a4:
Data protection is our highest priority:

• All data is processed in accordance with GDPR
• SSL-encrypted transmission
• Storage on German servers
• Disclosure only with your consent to potential employers
• Deletion possible on request at any time

More information can be found in our privacy policy.

technical.a1:
For documents we accept:

• PDF (recommended) - Maximum file size: 10 MB

Please ensure that:
• Documents are clearly legible
• No password-protected PDFs are used
• Translations are certified (if required)
technical.a2:
No problem! Here's how to reset it:

1. Go to the login page
2. Click on "Forgot password?"
3. Enter your email address
4. You will receive a reset link by email
5. Choose a new secure password

The link is valid for 24 hours. If you don't receive an email, check your spam folder.
technical.q3: How can I delete my account?
technical.a3:
You can delete your account yourself at any time:

1. Log in
2. Go to "Settings"
3. Scroll to "Delete Account"
4. Confirm with your password and "DELETE"

Warning: This action is irreversible. All your data, applications, and documents will be permanently deleted.
technical.q4: Who can I contact if I have problems?
technical.a4:
Our support team is happy to help:

Email: service@internationaljobplacement.com

Contact form: Via our contact page

We usually respond within 24-48 hours.
